events.event_description: First U.S. Minister Resident/Consul General to Siam presents credentials, establishing a U.S. legation.

evidence.quote: John Acoming Jr Halderman, Minister Resident/Consul General. Appointed: 1882-07-13. Credentials presented: 1882-10-23. Left post on 1885-04-01. Note: Commissioned to Siam.

events.event_description: Japanese forces occupied Bangkok; Thailand subsequently declared war on the United States, ending U.S. diplomatic representation. The minister was interned and eventually left post.

evidence.quote: Japanese forces occupied Bangkok 1941-12-08. Note: Thailand declared war on the United States on January 25, 1942. Peck, having been interned, left post on June 29, 1942.

events.event_description: U.S. Chargé d'Affaires presents credentials, re-establishing U.S. diplomatic representation in Thailand after the wartime break.

evidence.quote: Charles Woodruff Yost, Chargé d'Affaires. Credentials presented: 1946-01-05. Superseded 1946-07-04. Note: Not commissioned; letter of credence sent to Yost by telegram on October 16, 1945.

events.event_description: Edwin Forward Stanton promoted from Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary, elevating the U.S. mission from a legation to an embassy.

evidence.quote: Edwin Forward Stanton, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1946-04-27. Credentials presented: 1946-07-04. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary 1947-05-09.
